detailedExemplarData.title:
âHow do varying concentrations of eggshell powder adsorbent (1.00, 2.00, 3.00, 4.00, 5.00
grams) alongside the adsorption duration (5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30 minutes) affect its efficacy on the
concentration of manganese(VII) ions in wastewater?â

strengths: Accurate and effective communication of the environmental issue and research purpose throughout.
Research question is clearly stated, narrowly focused and revisited in discussion and conclusion.
Comprehensive methodology with justified selection of analytical techniques and clear presentation of variables and uncertainty treatment.
weaknesses: Minor typographical ambiguities in the bracketed RQ values.
Procedural details for pH control and particleâsize characterisation are omitted.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently meets the descriptors for the 5â6 band in all three strands.  The topic is communicated with clarity and sustained purpose, the RQ is well focused and linked to experimental design, and the methodology is complete with evidence of informed selection of methods.  The few minor lapses in wording and in reporting pH control do not undermine the overall coherence and rigour, justifying the top mark in this criterion.

strengths: Source material is applied clearly to underpin definitions, methods and data interpretation.
Subject knowledge is coherent, with correct deployment of adsorption theory, BeerâLambert law and redox chemistry.
Terminology and concepts (e.g. adsorption capacity, BET surface area, relative uncertainty) are used accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Integration of cited literature remains occasionally descriptive rather than critically engaged.
Some chemical equations (redox halfâequations) omit full chargeâbalance details.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While minor descriptive passages occur, the application of sources is overall highly relevant and appropriate; the candidate demonstrates clear, coherent subject knowledge and flawless use of technical terminology. These strengths align fully with the 5â6 band descriptors, meriting the maximum mark in this criterion.

strengths: Primary research is well planned with two independent data sets and uncertainty analysis, demonstrating good relevance to the RQ.
Analysis of trends is logical, with wellâsupported conclusions drawn from plotted models.
Discussion identifies random and systematic errors and proposes practical improvements, showing critical reflection.
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is limited by the absence of kinetic modelling or comparison with literature adsorption isotherms.
Discussion of wider implications and quantitative appraisal of key assumptions is only partial.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality clearly sits in the 7â9 band, with relevant, reliable data gathering. Analysis is solid (bordering 4â6 and 7â9 descriptors) and discussion shows partially critical evaluation. Taken together, the performance aligns best with the bottom of the 7â9 band, earning 7 marks out of 12.

strengths: Follows the extendedâessay structure rigorously, with systematic headings, embedded figures and numbered tables.
Pagination, word count and section headers are all present and correctly applied.
weaknesses: Inconsistent figure captions and occasional margin overrun in tables.
Reference list and citation style require uniform formatting.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation fully meets the topâband descriptors: structure and layout conventions are adhered to and all required elements (pagination, numbering, layout) are in place. Minor formatting inconsistencies do not impede readability, justifying the maximum 4 marks for presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay follows the required EE structure (title page, contents, introduction, methodology, results, evaluation, conclusion, references, appendix).  Headings and sub-headings are systematic, tables and figures are embedded and numbered.  Minor inconsistencies in figure captions and table numbering are present, but overall organisation clearly supports reading, fulfilling the top band.

The student explains the environmental problem of Mn(VII) contamination, justifies adsorption as a feasible treatment, and states a purpose that is sustained throughout the essay.  Background, definitions and global context are presented early, allowing the reader to understand why eggshell powder is investigated.  Minor wording confusion (duplicate values in the RQ) reduces absolute clarity, but overall communication is accurate, purposeful and maintained, consistent with the 5-6 band.

Graphs, fitted models and adsorption-capacity calculations provide clear analysis; trends are interpreted logically, and limitations are partially discussed.  However, the analysis remains mostly linear; there is no kinetic modelling or comparison with literature capacities, and some conclusions about pore wetting are asserted without further evidence.  Support is present but not thorough, corresponding to the middle of the 4-6 band.

The student gathers two substantial primary data sets with five trials each, applies uncertainty analysis and constructs calibration curves, ensuring data are largely reliable and relevant to the RQ.  The scope is adequate but limited to one adsorbent and one metal ion, preventing the comprehensive coverage expected for the highest band.  Thus the work exemplifies âgoodâ rather than âexcellentâ research, aligning to the lower end of the 7-9 band.

The essay demonstrates solid understanding of adsorption chemistry, BeerâLambert law and redox titration.  Calculations are correct, and terminology such as âadsorption capacityâ and âremoval efficiencyâ is used appropriately.  Nonetheless, mechanistic discussion (physisorption vs chemisorption) remains surface-level and some chemical equations are copied without deeper analysis.  This clear but partly effective knowledge sits at 4 in the 3-4 band.

Chemical vocabulary (e.g. âpermanganate ionâ, âBET surface areaâ, ârelative uncertaintyâ) is used accurately and consistently across sections, figures and data tables.  Only occasional typographical errors appear.  Hence the work meets the descriptors of accurate, consistent terminology; a mark of 5 in the top band is appropriate.

A full methodology is supplied: two preliminary methods (titration, calibration curve) to standardise Mn(VII); clear variable tables, safety, apparatus diagram, multiple trials, uncertainty treatment and two independent investigations.  Source selection (spectrophotometry vs titration) is justified.  Some procedural details (e.g. pH control, particle-size characterisation) are missing, so the selection is effective but not flawless, meriting 5 in the 5-6 band.

Pagination, word count and section headers are provided, but some formatting issues exist: RQ lists duplicated numbers, certain images are fuzzy, and table widths occasionally exceed margins.  Citation style is not fully consistent.  These minor lapses indicate âsome layout considerations missing or incorrectâ, meriting 3 (upper end of 1-2 band would be too harsh; 3 within 3-4 scale is most fitting).

The evaluation section lists strengths and weaknesses and proposes basic improvements, but reflections are brief and largely qualitative.  Alternative approaches (e.g., pH control, surface-area measurement) are acknowledged only superficially.  Discussion links back to the hypothesis, yet critical depth and engagement with wider implications are limited.  This meets the descriptor of âgood discussion with partially critical evaluationâ at the lower end of the 4-6 band.

Most cited literature supports definitions, health impacts, and adsorption theory; calibration-curve data and uncertainty tables are drawn from the studentâs own work.  Referenced material is generally appropriate, but integration is often descriptive and occasionally tangential.  Therefore the application is mostly relevant rather than consistently incisive, fitting the 3-4 band at the upper end.

The research question is explicitly stated, narrowly targets two controllable parameters (dosage and time) and is revisited in evaluation and conclusion.  Although the bracketed list inside the RQ contains some repeated/erroneous numbers, the focus actually implemented in the work (1â5 g; 5â30 min) is clear and well connected to data collection.  This degree of focus aligns with the top band, but the minor ambiguity warrants the lower mark within that band.

annotations.comment: The research question contains duplicated and misaligned parameter values, reducing clarity. Align the listed dosages and durations with those actually used (1â5 g; 5â30 min) to ensure precise focus.
The title is overly long and could be streamlined to improve reader engagement. Consider focusing on key variables (eggshell dosage, adsorption time) and outcome (Mn(VII) removal).
The word count is provided and falls within the 4000-word limit, satisfying IB requirements.
Minor inconsistencies in table of contents alignment and missing page numbers for sub-sections hinder navigation. Ensure all entries have correct page references and consistent formatting.
The introduction effectively sets the global context for heavy metal contamination, showing clear understanding of environmental impact.
Integration of cited literature in the background is mostly descriptive; deeper critical engagement with source limitations would strengthen the application.
The bracketed list in the research question on page 5 repeats values and contains typographical errors, undermining precision. Revise to match the implemented range used in experiments.
The stray handwritten equations are unrelated to the chemistry context. Remove or replace with relevant schematics to maintain coherence.
The physisorption vs chemisorption comparison table is clear and well-labelled, effectively supporting the background discussion.
Citation formatting in the background section is inconsistent (e.g., missing years, varied punctuation). Adopt a uniform referencing style.
Adsorption capacity formula $$q_e = \frac{(C_0 - C_e)V}{m}$$ is presented clearly with units defined. Excellent incorporation of quantitative concepts.
The redox half-equation (3) omits charge balance detail; include electron counts and spectator ions consistently to enhance chemical accuracy.
The pH control and ionic strength are not specified in methodology; these parameters significantly impact redox titration and adsorption reactions.
The discussion correctly acknowledges endpoint variability in titrations, demonstrating critical awareness of random error.
Reference list exhibits inconsistent formatting (author order, italics, periods). Apply a single citation style (eg APA or MLA) consistently.
Endpoint detection error is noted qualitatively; strengthen by quantifying its effect on concentration calculations via replication or spectroscopic endpoint.
Selection of 530 nm wavelength for spectrophotometry is well justified by the permanganate absorption peak.
Calibration curve data span is limited to 5â55 ppm, leading to non-linearity at extremes. Extend standard range or apply appropriate isotherm model.
Independent variables table omits pH and stirring speed, both critical to adsorption kinetics. Include these as controlled or independent factors.
Controlled variables table systematically lists key parameters, demonstrating careful experimental planning.
The labeled diagram of apparatus lacks a scale bar or dimensions, limiting reproducibility. Add measurements or annotations for clarity.
Procedure for grinding eggshells is clear but omits particle-size characterization, which affects adsorption performance. Conduct sieve or microscopy analysis.
Assumptions section acknowledges morphological uncertainties but could appraise their impact on adsorption capacity quantitatively.
Ethical considerations mention water use and waste reuse but lack reflection on how these influenced research decisions.
Hypothesis articulates expected trends concisely and connects adsorption time and dosage to capacity changes.
Figure captions for qualitative results are not sequentially referenced in text; ensure each figure is introduced by its number.
Uncertainty calculation for 500 cmÂ³ solution seems to average flask errors incorrectly; re-evaluate combined uncertainties using root-sum-square method.
Removal efficiency decreases linearly with time, but a kinetic model (e.g., pseudo-first order) would deepen analysis beyond linear interpretation.
Adsorption capacity values remain low (<1.3 mg/g); discuss these in relation to the eggshellâs BET surface area to contextualize performance.
Graph captions lack axis units; label both axes (e.g., mg/g, ppm, min, g) to ensure self-contained figures.
Evaluation section thoughtfully identifies random and systematic errors and proposes improvements, demonstrating critical reflection.
Conclusion restates findings but overextends claim of full Mn(VII) removal potential without further adsorbent comparison. Temper conclusions to data scope.
